source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt describing two longerons, five cross-members, four magnet-array mounting pads, two battery-tray mounting flanges, one LIM reaction-plate mounting pad, and the FSW seams/fasteners that join them; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the Global Hyperloop Competition 2.0 (collegiate-class IIT Madras) 2026 student pod primary chassis frame as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the ladder-style 7020-T6 aluminum spine as the physical collection of longerons, cross-members, bolt-on mounting pads/flanges, and the friction-stir-weld (FSW) seams and fasteners that join them. The original prompt treats the frame as 'monolithic' for analysis convenience (billet-machined + FSW-welded with the FSW seams modeled as continuous base-metal equivalent); this multipart decomposition exposes the same structure as its constituent manufactured parts for interface verification.

The assembly consists of seven part families: (A) two longitudinal longerons, each an 80 mm x 40 mm x 4 mm wall rectangular-section extruded/machined tube running the full 2200 mm length and spaced 500 mm apart on centerline; (B) five transverse cross-members, each a 60 mm x 40 mm x 3 mm wall rectangular-section tube at stations 0 / 550 / 1100 / 1650 / 2200 mm bridging the two longerons; (C) four magnet-array mounting pads on the underside of the longerons at stations 400 / 800 / 1400 / 1800 mm, each 100 x 80 x 8 mm with 4 M10 clearance holes on a 70 x 50 mm pattern; (D) two battery-tray mounting flanges on the upper longerons spanning stations 550-1100 and 1100-1650 mm, each 500 x 40 x 6 mm with 6 M8 clearance holes on a 250 mm pitch x 2 rows; (E) one LIM reaction-plate mounting pad centered at the 1100 mm station, 400 x 200 x 6 mm with 8 M10 through-holes; (F) FSW-seam material regions joining longerons-to-cross-members and longerons-to-pads (modeled as bonded tie surfaces with base-metal-equivalent properties per the original prompt's treatment); (G) the grade-8.8 steel fastener sets (M10 for magnet pads and LIM pad, M8 for battery flanges) that bolt the auxiliary subsystem interfaces to the frame (the fasteners that connect the frame to the battery, magnets, and LIM plate are outside the frame-as-a-part but are load-path participants on the assembly boundary and are included here for connection verification).

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: overall ladder frame 2200 mm long x 600 mm wide x 120 mm tall; longerons 80x40x4 wall, spaced 500 mm; cross-members 60x40x3 wall at 5 stations; magnet pads 100x80x8 at 4 stations with 11 mm (M10 clearance) holes on 70x50 pattern; battery flanges 500x40x6 with 9 mm (M8 clearance) holes on 250 mm pitch; LIM pad 400x200x6 with 8 M10 through-holes; bounding envelope 2200 x 600 x 120 mm.

Material inherits verbatim: 7020-T6 aluminum for all primary frame parts (E 71 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2780 kg/m^3, yield 290 MPa, UTS 350 MPa, endurance 90 MPa at 5e8 cycles, weldable); FSW seams treated as base-metal-equivalent per the original prompt; fasteners are grade-8.8 steel (standard hyperloop-class practice; not specified by the original sample).

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 propulsion at 0.5g longitudinal (980 N thrust at LIM pad + distributed subsystem weights, magnet-pad levitation-reaction constraints); LC2 emergency braking at 2g (3920 N decel at LIM pad + 2g forward inertial load on all attached masses); LC3 vacuum differential 10 kPa upward body load on 1 m^2 projected bottom + 25 C thermal delta restrained by LIM bolt pattern.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) peak von Mises under LC1 <= 145 MPa (yield/2.0); (R2) peak von Mises under LC2/LC3 <= 193 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) first six natural frequencies (frame + 80 kg battery + 30 kg magnets + 25 kg LIM lumped at their pads) >= 25 Hz; (R4) first-mode buckling load factor under LC2 >= 3.0; (R5) frame assembly mass <= 35 kg; (R6) battery-tray mounting flange deflection under LC2 <= 2.0 mm. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: connection DCR at FSW-seam interfaces (IF1-IF3 bonded) and at bolted interfaces (IF4-IF6 bolted) must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Thermal analysis is reduced to LC3 thermal growth; fluid and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (each longeron, each cross-member, each magnet pad, each battery flange, the LIM pad) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..G) and instance_index, plus a report giving max von Mises per case, first six frequencies, first buckling load factor, battery-flange deflection, assembly mass, and connection DCR at each interface.
short_prompt:
Design a hyperloop student pod frame as a multi-part aluminium-extrusion welded assembly (bottom pan, side rails, cross members, bolted brackets).

Envelope 2200 x 600 x 120 mm; total mass <= 35 kg; flange flatness <= 2.0 mm. Material Al 7020-T6 yield 290 MPa.

Load cases: LC1 0.5g vertical + 10 kPa differential + 25 C ambient (allowable <= 145 MPa); LC2 2g worst-case (allowable <= 193 MPa); LC3 modal separation.

Pass/fail: stress <= 145 MPa (LC1) / <= 193 MPa (LC2); first modal frequency >= 25 Hz; buckling load factor >= 3.0; FEM at assembled level; welded and bolted DCR <= 1.0.

prompt.title: collegiate-class IIT Madras hyperloop pod primary chassis frame (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 7020-T6 ladder-frame assembly: 2 longerons (A) + 5 cross-members (B) + 4 magnet pads (C) + 2 battery flanges (D) + 1 LIM reaction pad (E), joined by FSW seams (F) with bolted subsystem fastener sets (G). Overall 2200x600x120 mm envelope, 35 kg mass budget. Verified at assembly level: peak von Mises <= 145 MPa (LC1) / 193 MPa (LC2, LC3), first six modes >= 25 Hz, first buckling factor >= 3.0 under LC2, battery-flange deflection <= 2 mm, connection DCR <= 1.0 at all FSW seams and bolted subsystem interfaces.

parts.name: longeron
parts.role: 80x40x4 wall rectangular-section 7020-T6 tube running the full 2200 mm length; carries primary bending + axial flow between bumpers and LIM pad

parts.name: cross_member
parts.role: 60x40x3 wall rectangular-section transverse brace at 0 / 550 / 1100 / 1650 / 2200 mm stations; carries lateral/torsional loads between the two longerons

parts.name: magnet_pad
parts.role: 100x80x8 mounting pad on underside of outer longeron at 400/800/1400/1800 mm stations; transmits levitation-reaction vertical + lateral load into the longerons through 4 M10 bolts each

parts.name: battery_flange
parts.role: 500x40x6 mounting flange on upper longeron between stations 550-1100 and 1100-1650; carries 80 kg battery pack distributed weight + 2g inertial load into the longerons through 6 M8 bolts each

parts.name: lim_reaction_pad
parts.role: 400x200x6 mounting pad centered at 1100 mm station; transmits LIM thrust/brake (up to 3920 N at 2g) and 10 kPa vacuum differential reaction into cross-member 3 and the longerons through 8 M10 bolts

parts.name: fsw_seam_region
parts.role: friction-stir-weld seam tie region between longerons and cross-members and between longerons and pads; modeled as bonded tie surface with 7020-T6 base-metal-equivalent properties per the original prompt's FSW treatment

geometric_constraints.thickness_mm: nominal (material-level feature, not a separate solid in all CAD workflows)

parts.name: subsystem_bolt_set
parts.role: grade-8.8 steel M10 and M8 bolt + nut + washer sets that attach the battery pack, magnet arrays, and LIM plate to the frame through pads C/D/E; connection reactions are verified on the assembly boundary
parts.count_note: 4 pads x 4 M10 + 2 flanges x 6 M8 + 1 LIM pad x 8 M10 = 16 + 12 + 8 = 36 bolts; rounded up to 40 to cover washers and any stiffener fasteners

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: pod coordinates: +x forward along track, +y starboard, +z up; frame origin at rear-cross-member outer-longeron starboard intersection

boundary_conditions.magnet_pads: vertical + lateral levitation-reaction distributed over C part contact patches
boundary_conditions.battery_flange_bolts: tied fastener pairs at the 6 M8 locations per flange carrying the 80 kg battery distributed weight + 2g inertial load
boundary_conditions.lim_pad_bolts: tied fastener pairs at the 8 M10 locations carrying thrust/brake longitudinal load
boundary_conditions.fsw_seams: bonded tie surfaces between A-B, A-C, A-D, A-E (base-metal equivalent)

prompt.objective: primary: pass all six R-criteria under assembled_only FEM check. secondary: minimize assembly mass below 35 kg.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: FSW bonded tie surfaces (IF1-IF3) and bolted subsystem interfaces (IF4-IF6) per grade-8.8 bolt capacity table and 7020-T6 FSW joint allowables
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: mixed shell (longeron/cross-member walls) + solid (mounting pads) + tie surfaces (FSW seams)

interfaces.load_path: FSW seam between longerons and cross-members; transfers shear + bending flow across the ladder nodes
FSW seam between longeron undersides and magnet mounting pads; transfers levitation-reaction vertical/lateral load into the longerons
FSW seam between longeron upper surface and battery-tray flange; transfers battery weight + 2g inertial load
FSW seam between mid-cross-member (station 1100) and LIM reaction-pad; transfers LIM thrust into the cross-member
4 M10 grade-8.8 bolts per magnet pad carrying levitation-reaction load from the external magnet arrays into the frame
6 M8 grade-8.8 bolts per battery flange carrying 80 kg battery + 2g inertial load
8 M10 grade-8.8 bolts at LIM reaction pad carrying propulsion/brake thrust + vacuum differential reaction

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original prompt already describes the frame as an FSW-welded + billet-machined construction with separate longerons, cross-members, and mounting pads; 'monolithic for this prompt' is an analysis convenience, not a manufacturing reality.
notes.speculation_caveats: Cross-member count of 5 and bolt counts (4/6/8 per pad) are inherited verbatim. Exact FSW bead geometry and fastener grade (grade-8.8) are speculated.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
